author: Octavio Peña
title: Huevos a la Diabla
ingredients: ¼ white onion, split into layers
¼ white onion, split into layers

instructions:
Bring a medium pot of water to a boil. Add the eggs, reduce the heat to low and set a timer for 11 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare a large bowl of ice water. Once eggs are cooked, transfer them to the ice water and allow to chill, about 5 minutes. Dump out the egg-boiling water and refill with fresh water. Bring to a boil. Add guajillo and arbol chiles to the pot. Soak, off heat, until chiles soften, about 5 minutes.
Peel the eggs, cut them in half and scoop the yolks into the blender. Chop the egg whites into bite-size pieces and set aside in a large bowl.
Heat oil in a medium skillet over high heat. Once oil is shimmering, add onion, tomato, and garlic. Stir to coat in oil. Cook, stirring frequently, until charred, about 4 minutes. Transfer to the blender along with the softened chiles and ¼ cup of soaking liquid. Blend on high speed until the salsa is smooth and creamy. Salt to taste. Set aside.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add chorizo and cook, breaking up into smaller pieces with a spatula, until meat is crispy, about 8 minutes. Transfer the chorizo and any rendered fat to the bowl with the egg whites. Add salsa and toss until thoroughly coated. Serve over tostadas and top with chives.

Once eggs are cooked, transfer them to the ice water and allow to chill, about 5 minutes. Dump out the egg-boiling water and refill with fresh water. Bring to a boil. Add guajillo and arbol chiles to the pot. Soak, off heat, until chiles soften, about 5 minutes.
Peel the eggs, cut them in half and scoop the yolks into the blender. Chop the egg whites into bite-size pieces and set aside in a large bowl.

Heat oil in a medium skillet over high heat. Once oil is shimmering, add onion, tomato, and garlic. Stir to coat in oil. Cook, stirring frequently, until charred, about 4 minutes. Transfer to the blender along with the softened chiles and ¼ cup of soaking liquid. Blend on high speed until the salsa is smooth and creamy. Salt to taste. Set aside.

Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add chorizo and cook, breaking up into smaller pieces with a spatula, until meat is crispy, about 8 minutes. Transfer the chorizo and any rendered fat to the bowl with the egg whites. Add salsa and toss until thoroughly coated. Serve over tostadas and top with chives.
description: Hardboiled eggs and chorizo are tossed in a spicy, yolk-based salsa roja and served on a tostada with chives.
